perturbation.original_text: In any action that is brought to enforce or interpret this Agreement, the prevailing party or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to recover their reasonable attorneys’ and paralegal fees and expenses incurred in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: In any action that is brought to enforce or interpret this Agreement, neither party shall be entitled to recover attorneys’ or paralegal fees and expenses incurred in connection therewith, regardless of who prevails.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that neither party can recover attorney's fees, regardless of who wins the case. This directly contradicts the original text, which allowed the prevailing party to recover these fees. This change makes Section 10 contradictory to itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In any action that is brought to enforce or interpret this Agreement, neither party shall be entitled to recover attorneys’ or paralegal fees and expenses incurred in connection therewith, regardless of who prevails. If a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, institute any action or proceeding to enforce the provisions of this Agreement, Employee hereby waives all claims or defenses that a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, have an adequate remedy at law, and Employee shall not urge in any such action or proceeding the claim or defense that such a remedy at law exists.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'neither party shall be entitled to recover attorneys’ or paralegal fees and expenses incurred in connection therewith, regardless of who prevails', which directly contradicts the contradicted text that contains the exact same clause. According to the explanation given, this change makes Section 10 contradictory to itself. Thus, there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be terminated, amended or modified without the prior written consent of the Bank’s President and Chief Executive Officer and Employee.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated, amended or modified by either party providing 30 days written notice to the other party. The consent of the Bank’s President and Chief Executive Officer is not required.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by allowing either party to unilaterally terminate, amend, or modify the agreement with 30 days' notice, directly contradicting the original requirement for mutual written consent from both the Bank's President/CEO and the Employee. This contradicts the clause itself, as the section now is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated, amended or modified by either party providing 30 days written notice to the other party. The consent of the Bank’s President and Chief Executive Officer is not required. No failure or delay by a Banking Organization in exercising any right or remedy under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any waiver by a Banking Organization under this Agreement operate or be construed as a continuing waiver or a waiver of any subsequent breach or noncompliance hereunder. No single or partial exercise of any right or remedy by a Banking Organization shall preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right or remedy. Any waiver by a Banking Organization under this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by the Bank’s President and Chief Executive Officer. A waiver shall operate only as to the specific term or condition waived and will not constitute a waiver for the future or act on anything other than that which is specifically waived.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the written consent of both the Bank’s President/CEO and the Employee for any termination, amendment, or modification. The changed text allows either party to unilaterally make such changes with 30 days' notice, without requiring the consent of the Bank’s President/CEO. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and since the changed text contradicts the original text, there is a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition: 'Inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'

perturbation.original_text: The covenants set forth in this Agreement are essential terms and conditions to a Banking Organization employing or continuing to employ Employee, and shall be construed as independent of any other obligations or agreements between Employee and a Banking Organization.
perturbation.changed_text: The covenants set forth in this Agreement are not essential terms and conditions to a Banking Organization employing or continuing to employ Employee, and shall be construed as dependent on other obligations or agreements between Employee and a Banking Organization.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by stating the covenants are *not* essential and are dependent on other agreements. This contradicts the original language and undermines the enforceability of the non-compete and non-solicitation clauses, impacting the essence of the agreement. This directly creates an in-text contradiction as the section has two opposing statements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The covenants set forth in this Agreement are not essential terms and conditions to a Banking Organization employing or continuing to employ Employee, and shall be construed as dependent on other obligations or agreements between Employee and a Banking Organization. The existence of any claim or cause of action Employee may have against a Banking Organization, including, but not limited to, the alleged material breach by a Banking Organization of any agreement with Employee, shall not constitute a defense to the enforcement by a Banking Organization of the covenants and obligations in this Agreement and shall not relieve Employee of his or her obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the covenants are *not* essential and are dependent on other agreements. The contradicted text includes this same statement, implying a contradiction with what was originally written in the document previously, where the covenants were essential. This undermines the enforceability of the non-compete and non-solicitation clauses, impacting the essence of the agreement. Therefore, the altered text causes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees and acknowledges that the covenants, restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement, including Sections 5 and 6 , are reasonable and necessary to protect a Banking Organization. Employee agrees that the covenants, restrictions and obligations will not affect Employee’s ability to make a living.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee agrees and acknowledges that the covenants, restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement, including Sections 5 and 6, are unreasonable and unnecessary to protect a Banking Organization. Employee agrees that the covenants, restrictions and obligations will significantly affect Employee’s ability to make a living.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by now stating that the covenants are *unreasonable* and *unnecessary*, and also that they *will* affect the employee's ability to earn a living, directly opposing the original statement. This creates internal conflict in Section 19, making the enforceability of the covenants questionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Employee agrees and acknowledges that the covenants, restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement, including Sections 5 and 6, are unreasonable and unnecessary to protect a Banking Organization. Employee agrees that the covenants, restrictions and obligations will significantly affect Employee’s ability to make a living.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the covenants are 'unreasonable and unnecessary' and 'will significantly affect the Employee's ability to make a living.' The contradicted text repeats the same statement which directly opposes the original text, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Employee affirms and represents that performance of his/her job duties and responsibilities for a Financial Institution does not, and will not, breach any agreement or covenant not to Solicit business, compete in business, or keep in confidence proprietary or confidential information acquired in confidence or in trust prior to Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee affirms and represents that performance of his/her job duties and responsibilities for a Financial Institution does, and will, breach an agreement or covenant not to Solicit business, compete in business, or keep in confidence proprietary or confidential information acquired in confidence or in trust prior to Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text now claims that the employee's performance *will* breach prior agreements, which contradicts the initial statement that it will *not*. This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 22 itself regarding the employee's compliance with previous obligations and throws into question his/her employment validity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Employee affirms and represents that performance of his/her job duties and responsibilities for a Financial Institution does, and will, breach an agreement or covenant not to Solicit business, compete in business, or keep in confidence proprietary or confidential information acquired in confidence or in trust prior to Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution. Employee further represents that he/she has not retained any documents or information relating to Employee’s prior employers (other than Heritage or HopFed) that may be considered confidential or proprietary information and that Employee has not disclosed or used, and will not disclose or use, any information relating to his or her prior employer(s) in connection with Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the employee's performance will *not* breach prior agreements. The changed text states the employee's performance *will* breach prior agreements. This directly contradicts the employee's compliance with previous obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text which affirms that their performance will breach agreements. The explanation provided also indicates this creates a contradiction, making the answer YES.
